_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about the role of political parties and independent representatives in Australian democracy, including how elections and referendums work.

_source.w: We are learning about the role of political parties and independent representatives in Australian democracy, including how elections and referendums work.
y: Political parties and independents shape the policies that affect our everyday lives.
Understanding how elections work prepares us to be informed voters when the time comes.
Referendums give citizens direct power over constitutional change — knowing how they work is essential civic knowledge.
eg: Comparing the platforms of two major political parties on an issue like education or the environment.
Investigating the role of independent representatives and minor parties in parliament.
Examining how preferential voting works in the House of Representatives versus proportional representation in the Senate.
Analysing a past referendum — why it was held, what was proposed, and whether it succeeded.
Discussing why voter turnout matters for the health of a democracy.
Simulating a mock election with different parties, campaigns, and voting procedures.
